run_summary.summary: Catch-up run covering Apr 18–20 — previous Brandi sessions ran on a detached git HEAD and never reached main or the database. Two new deals: Linkind ($2K IG Reel, concept back-and-forth continuing) and Bedsure ($4.5K quote, still no response from Iris). Key urgents: SOJOS Reel 1 draft due Apr 23 (tomorrow). Flexsteel/Popfly hit a snag Apr 20 evening — campaign application requires full IG business manager access and Kenny pushed back; Ian hasn't responded yet. Ollie's Group 2 hard deadline Apr 29. FYI: xTool (Makeblock) reached out Apr 14–20 about an in-kind collab for a $10K CO2 laser — Liz declined Apr 20 (no cash budget, prioritizing paid deals only). No card created.

suggestions.message: Reel 1 draft is due TOMORROW (Apr 23) to Ruby at marketing202@sojosvision.com! Final requirements per Ruby's Apr 20 email: video ≥10 sec (updated from earlier ≥15 sec brief), close-ups of sunglasses ≥ half the video, outdoor/car background, tag @sojosvision, hashtags #sojos and #amazonsunglasses, include MISSLIZSOJOS (10% off) in caption. Ruby approves within 24 hours of receiving draft.

Popfly campaign application is BLOCKED — platform requires full 'managerial access' to Liz's Instagram business account (via FB Business Manager) to apply. Kenny pushed back Apr 20 at 6:09pm, asking Ian if they can narrow the permission scope or use whitelisting instead. Ian hasn't responded yet. Needs to be resolved before the application can be completed. Ian also asked what time they plan to head to Steinhafels (store needs advance notice) — no answer yet.

Group 2 content hard deadline is April 29 — 9 days from today. Bring Permission to Film Slip to the store. Draft must be submitted via campaign manager before posting. Don't make it sound like a sale.

Liz sent a detailed concept breakdown Apr 20 (Top 3 Tips theme, Linkind as #1, lights 50%+ screen time). Waiting on brand's final concept approval. Original Apr 15 deadline passed with no confirmed extension in writing — if no response by Apr 22, follow up.
Liz sent $4,500 bundle quote to Iris (MakeWonder MCN) Apr 13, followed up Apr 20 — still no response. If Iris doesn't reply by Apr 23, worth one more nudge or moving on.

FYI — previous Brandi runs from Apr 18–20 were committed to a detached git HEAD instead of main. 29 commits are orphaned and never reached origin/main or Supabase. To review: run 'git log --oneline b52c6bf --not main' from the repo. Notable orphaned code changes: 'Label last contact date and add next action date to deal cards', 'Always log activity when Brandi changes deal fields', 'Enforce forward-only stage moves in sync route'. Kenny may want to cherry-pick those into main.

deals.next_action: Film Reel 1 and submit draft to Ruby for approval (due Apr 23)
deals.notes: [Apr 20]: Ruby confirmed signed contract received + resent final Reel 1 requirements: video ≥10 sec (updated from ≥15 sec in earlier brief), close-up shots of sunglasses ≥ half video, outdoor/car background, tag @sojosvision, hashtags #sojos and #amazonsunglasses, MISSLIZSOJOS 10% off code in caption. Submit draft to Ruby before posting — 24hr approval turnaround.

deals.next_action: Follow up on concept approval if no response by Apr 22 — ready to film once approved
deals.notes:
[Apr 20 PM]: Brand requested time breakdown for concept — Liz replied with detailed plan: Top 3 Tips theme, Linkind as #1 tip, lights featured 50%+ of video (assembly, app screen recordings, day/night footage, key features). Liz confirmed she can't provide exact timing without shooting but guarantees lights will be main focus. Awaiting final concept approval.
[Apr 20 AM]: Liz followed up asking if concept was approved; said ready to film today.
[Apr 14]: Platform requested detailed concept by following morning. Liz pushed back — requirements too detailed for $2K, product not received, out of town Apr 17–20.
[Apr 6]: Contract revisions — Liz asked to extend posting deadline (was Apr 15) and cap revisions at 2 max. Platform said 'no problem' but no written confirmation of new deadline.
[Apr 1]: Contract sent for review.
[Mar 31]: Deal agreed via socialmediacollabs.com — $2,000 for 1x 60–90 sec dedicated IG Reel reviewing Linkind SP6 Smart Solar Pathway Lights.
deals.activity_note: Apr 20: New deal — $2,000 Linkind IG Reel via socialmediacollabs.com. Agreed Mar 31, contract revised Apr 6. Concept approval pending; original Apr 15 deadline passed with no confirmed extension.

deals.next_action: Follow up with Iris if no reply by Apr 23
deals.notes:
[Apr 20]: Liz followed up to confirm Iris received rate quote — still no response since Apr 13.
[Apr 13]: Liz quoted $4,500 bundle (1 IG Reel + 14-day link in bio + 90-day paid usage rights). Itemized: $2,500/Reel + $1,000/30-day paid usage. CC'd Kenny.
[Apr 10–13]: Iris from MakeWonder MCN (official Bedsure partner agency) reached out about paid collab + free Bedsure sheet set. Deliverables: 1 IG Reel, 90-day paid ads usage, brand tag + hashtag, 2-week link in bio.

deals.notes:
[Apr 20 PM]: Popfly campaign application hit a snag — platform requires 'full managerial access' to Liz's Instagram business account (via FB Business Manager) to apply. Kenny emailed Ian pushing back: scope too broad, concerned about accidental algo changes, willing to provide insights/whitelisting but not full managerial rights. Waiting for Ian's response. Ian also asked what time they plan to visit Steinhafels (no answer yet — store needs advance notice).
[Apr 20 AM]: Signed up for Popfly — welcome email received. Also planning to apply to HydroFlask campaign on marketplace.
[Apr 17–20 thread]: Phase 3 filming locked for May 13–14 — Kenny's dad has pacemaker follow-up May 11 in Naples; filming with him after he returns May 12. Ian Saunders at Popfly (ian@popfly.com) accepted the timeline. Location: Steinhafels, Downers Grove IL. Agreement deadline: end of May for post.

run_summary.summary: Apr 21 AM scan — one meaningful update. Ian Saunders responded to Kenny's Popfly access pushback at 8:42 PM Apr 20: he says 'no brand will have access to this (neither Popfly)' and suspects it may be a bug — he wants to check with his dev team. Asked Kenny to send a screenshot of the access prompt. Ball is back in our court. All other threads (Linkind concept, Bedsure follow-up, xTool decline) were already captured in yesterday's run.

suggestions.message: Ian responded to Kenny's Popfly access concern — he says no brand OR Popfly would have that access and thinks it might be a platform bug. He needs a screenshot of exactly where Kenny is seeing the 'managerial access' prompt. Send that screenshot to ian@popfly.com today so he can investigate with his dev team and unblock the application.
